</div><div class="mw-content-ltr mw-parser-output" lang="en" dir="ltr"><div class="shortdescription nomobile noexcerpt noprint searchaux" style="display:none">Browser extension to circumvent paywalls</div>
<p class="mw-empty-elt">

</p>

<p><b>Bypass Paywalls Clean</b> (<b>BPC</b>) is a <a href="/wiki/Free_and_open-source" class="mw-redirect" title="Free and open-source">free and open-source</a> web <a href="/wiki/Browser_extension" title="Browser extension">browser extension</a> that circumvents <a href="/wiki/Paywall" title="Paywall">paywalls</a>.
</p>
<meta property="mw:PageProp/toc">
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Characteristics">Characteristics</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Bypass_Paywalls_Clean&action=edit&section=1" title="Edit section: Characteristics"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Developed by magnolia1234, the extension uses techniques such as clearing <a href="/wiki/HTTP_cookies" class="mw-redirect" title="HTTP cookies">cookies</a> and showing content from <a href="/wiki/Web_archives" class="mw-redirect" title="Web archives">web archives</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-Maxwell_Aug_2024_2-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Maxwell_Aug_2024-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-Gigazine_2022_3-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Gigazine_2022-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The extension supports <a href="/wiki/Mozilla_Firefox" class="mw-redirect" title="Mozilla Firefox">Mozilla Firefox</a> and <a href="/wiki/Google_Chrome" title="Google Chrome">Google Chrome</a>. Other browsers such as <a href="/wiki/Safari_(web_browser)" title="Safari (web browser)">Safari</a> and <a href="/wiki/Brave_(web_browser)" title="Brave (web browser)">Brave</a> are supported with third-party adblockers as long as the BPC filterlist and the userscript are imported.<sup id="cite_ref-Gigazine_2022_3-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Gigazine_2022-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="DMCA_takedown_notices">DMCA takedown notices</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Bypass_Paywalls_Clean&action=edit&section=2" title="Edit section: DMCA takedown notices"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>Due to a conflict with <a href="/wiki/Google" title="Google">Google</a>'s rules, Bypass Paywalls Clean is not published on the <a href="/wiki/Chrome_Web_Store" title="Chrome Web Store">Chrome Web Store</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-Gigazine_2022_3-2" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Gigazine_2022-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Bypass Paywalls Clean was published on the <a href="/wiki/Add-ons_for_Firefox" class="mw-redirect" title="Add-ons for Firefox">Add-ons for Firefox</a> website until a <a href="/wiki/Online_Copyright_Infringement_Liability_Limitation_Act" title="Online Copyright Infringement Liability Limitation Act">DMCA takedown notice</a> was leveled against the Firefox extension in February 2023.<sup id="cite_ref-4" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-4"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> The extension was originally released on <a href="/wiki/GitLab" title="GitLab">GitLab</a> before it was removed in April 2024, when a DMCA takedown notice was filed against its <a href="/wiki/Source_code_repository" class="mw-redirect" title="Source code repository">source code repository</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-5" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-5"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Subsequently, magnolia1234 migrated Bypass Paywalls Clean to <a href="/wiki/GitHub" title="GitHub">GitHub</a>, where it was targeted by another DMCA takedown notice submitted by the <a href="/wiki/News_Media_Alliance" title="News Media Alliance">News Media Alliance</a>, resulting in GitHub restricting downloads of the software and its 3,879 <a href="/wiki/Fork_(software_development)" title="Fork (software development)">forks</a> in August 2024.<sup id="cite_ref-Maxwell_Aug_2024_2-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Maxwell_Aug_2024-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-6" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-6"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>  
</p><p>The project is currently being maintained and released on GitFlic, a Git <a href="/wiki/Forge_(software)" title="Forge (software)">forge</a> by Russian technology company <a href="/wiki/RusBITech" title="RusBITech">RusBITech</a>.
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="See_also">See also</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Bypass_Paywalls_Clean&action=edit&section=3" title="Edit section: See also"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
